name: WordPress (npx)
description: Connect to a WordPress site through its remote MCP endpoint to read and manage posts, pages, users, media, and more. Authenticate with a WordPress application password (username + application password) against a self-hosted or Jetpack-connected site.

inputs.label: WordPress Site URL
inputs.description: Full WordPress MCP endpoint URL, e.g. https://your-site.com/wp-json/mcp/mcp-adapter-default-server. A bare site root URL (https://your-site.com) is also accepted and resolves to the legacy /wp-json/wp/v2/wpmcp endpoint for backwards compatibility.

inputs.label: WordPress Username
inputs.description: The WordPress account username that owns the application password used for authentication.

inputs.label: Application Password
inputs.description: A WordPress application password generated for this account. This is not your normal login password.
inputs.placeholder: xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx

obtain.instructions:
1. Log in to your WordPress admin dashboard
2. Go to Users > Profile (or your account's profile page)
3. Scroll to the 'Application Passwords' section
4. Enter a name (e.g. 'McpMux') and click 'Add New Application Password'
5. Copy the generated password (you'll see it only once)

Note: Application Passwords are a self-hosted WordPress feature. On WordPress.com sites you may instead need a WordPress.com API token or OAuth.

auth.instructions: Authenticate with your WordPress username plus an application password. Create the application password under Users > Profile > Application Passwords in your WordPress admin dashboard. On WordPress.com sites, a WordPress.com API token or OAuth may be required instead.
